summary: Implement Option A as a shared Tailwind-powered UI system for independently deployed ONCE apps.
delegationNotes: Each task is scoped so it can be handed to a subagent independently. Respect dependencies before implementation. Prefer one writer at a time for files that overlap, especially pkg/ui and justfile.

tasks.title: Add Tailwind toolchain and build commands
tasks.description: Introduce a minimal Tailwind build workflow, likely using bunx/@tailwindcss/cli, and wire it into just commands without adding a frontend app framework.
acceptanceCriteria: A repeatable command compiles pkg/ui/styles/once.css to pkg/ui/static/once.css
justfile includes a CSS build command and quality/build flow documents when to run it
Generated CSS is deterministic and suitable for embedding in Go binaries
No React/Vue/Svelte or browser app framework is introduced

tasks.title: Define Option A Tailwind theme tokens
tasks.description: Encode the Calm Minimal palette, spacing, radius, shadows, content widths, and typography as Tailwind theme values and/or CSS variables.
acceptanceCriteria: Theme exposes reusable values for background, surface, border, text, muted text, primary, accent, and danger
Theme exposes app-wide content width and header sizing tokens
Generated CSS supports normal Tailwind utilities plus ONCE-specific tokens
Tokens match the approved Option A visual direction

tasks.title: Create shared Tailwind component classes
tasks.description: Add @layer components classes for repeated UI primitives: buttons, cards, form controls, empty states, alerts, and markdown/prose styling.
acceptanceCriteria: Classes exist for once-btn variants, once-card, once-input, once-textarea, once-empty, and once-alert
Markdown content has shared styling, either md-content or prose/once-prose
Focus, hover, disabled, and danger states are covered
One-off layout can still use regular Tailwind utilities

tasks.title: Create pkg/ui renderer package
tasks.description: Add a shared Go package around html/template, embed.FS, shared layout templates, common page data, and static asset serving.
acceptanceCriteria: pkg/ui exposes App, Page, Renderer, NewRenderer, and Render APIs
Shared templates are embedded and parsed with app templates at startup
Template names are namespaced to avoid collisions
Compiled CSS can be served from a reserved route such as /assets/once/once.css
Package has basic tests for parsing and rendering

tasks.title: Build shared layout and error templates
tasks.description: Implement the independent-app Option A document shell: app header, page container, CSS asset link, optional shared error page, and small template partials where useful.
acceptanceCriteria: Layout has app identity but no cross-app navigation
Layout links to the compiled Tailwind CSS asset
Error template can be reused by Notes, Files, and Blog
Shared templates remain simple and readable

tasks.title: Migrate Notes templates to pkg/ui
tasks.description: Move Notes off its local Tailwind CDN base template and onto the shared renderer/layout/classes while preserving existing routes and behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Notes pages render through pkg/ui shared layout
Tailwind CDN script is removed from Notes templates
Index, view, form, and error pages match Option A direction
Existing handlers/routes continue to work
go test ./... passes

tasks.title: Normalize Notes view models and page titles
tasks.description: Refine template data after migration: avoid title duplication, consider page-specific view models, and keep sanitized markdown handling explicit.
acceptanceCriteria: Browser titles do not duplicate app names
Template data is clear and typed enough for index/view/form/error pages
template.HTML is only used for sanitized markdown output
Existing tests pass or are updated appropriately

tasks.title: Add renderer and UI regression tests
tasks.description: Add tests that catch broken template parsing, missing asset links, unsafe rendering regressions, and major Notes render failures.
acceptanceCriteria: pkg/ui tests parse/render a fixture app page
Notes render tests cover index, view, form, and error pages
Tests verify shared CSS link is present
go test ./... passes

tasks.title: Document UI system usage for future apps
tasks.description: Document how a new app should use pkg/ui, Tailwind tokens, shared components, and the CSS build command.
acceptanceCriteria: Docs explain independent app identity plus shared visual system
Docs include a minimal new-app template example
Docs explain when to use shared component classes vs raw Tailwind utilities
Docs mention the Tailwind build command

tasks.title: Prepare Files and Blog adoption notes
tasks.description: Create short follow-up plans for how Files and Blog should use the same UI system once those apps become real implementations.
acceptanceCriteria: Files plan maps list/upload/detail/delete states to shared components
Blog plan maps post index/view/editor states to shared components
Plans identify any app-specific component needs before implementation

tasks.title: Final visual and code review
tasks.description: Review the complete migration for consistency, idiomatic Go, Tailwind usage, accessibility, and regressions.
acceptanceCriteria: Reviewer confirms no accidental cross-app shell/nav was introduced
Reviewer confirms Tailwind setup is not overcomplicated
Reviewer confirms templates are readable and safe
just quality passes
